title: Optimising Lists and Images
description: Improve list performance and handle images efficiently.

slides.title: List Performance Recap
slides.content:
# Use FlatList or SectionList

For long lists, always use `FlatList` â€“ it virtualises content. But even `FlatList` can be optimised:
- Provide `getItemLayout` if items have fixed size
- Use `initialNumToRender` wisely
- Avoid anonymous functions in `renderItem` (use `useCallback`)
- Set `maxToRenderPerBatch` and `windowSize` appropriately

slides.title: Optimised FlatList Example
Image Optimisation
slides.content:
## Images Are Heavy

- Resize images to the display size â€“ don't download 4K images for a thumbnail.
- Use `resizeMode` appropriately.
- Cache images with libraries like `expo-image` or `react-native-fast-image`.
- For remote images, set `width` and `height` to avoid layout shifts.

Example with `react-native-fast-image` (not Expoâ€‘friendly, but popular):
```jsx
import FastImage from 'react-native-fast-image';

<FastImage
  style={{ width: 200, height: 200 }}
  source={{ uri: 'https://example.com/image.jpg', priority: FastImage.priority.normal }}
  resizeMode={FastImage.resizeMode.contain}
/>
```

quizData.question: What does `getItemLayout` do for FlatList?

options.text: It provides fixed item dimensions to skip measurement
It arranges items in a grid

quizData.explanation: `getItemLayout` tells FlatList the exact size and position of each item, avoiding measurement overhead.
